performance_summary: No performance data yet. System just initialized. First run in progress.

directives.directive: First run: establish baseline. Test the most fundamentally sound hypothesis you can think of — not the most popular, the most logically defensible from first principles.
directives.why: We need a starting point that is grounded in economic reality, not cargo-culted from retail trading forums.
directives.success_metric: At least one experiment logged with full metrics within the first 3 sessions.

directives.directive: Do NOT test generic RSI overbought/oversold as your first hypothesis. That is the first thing every beginner tries. Find something less obvious.
directives.why: If millions of traders already act on a signal, it is priced in. We need signals with information asymmetry.
directives.success_metric: Hypothesis is something a typical retail trader would not immediately think of.

directives.directive: Prioritize testing combinations of signals from DIFFERENT categories — e.g. a technical signal confirmed by a fundamental signal. Single-source signals are fragile.
directives.why: First principles: a price move predicted by both technical structure AND fundamental catalyst is more reliable than either alone.
directives.success_metric: Tested hypothesis uses at least 2 signal categories.

if_no_proven_strategy_in_48hrs: Escalate hypothesis ambition. Stop testing incremental variations. Test something radically different.
red_lines: Never deploy a strategy with fewer than 50 OOS trades
Never recycle a hypothesis that already failed — log it and move on
Never report a win rate without the confidence interval
